title: Myosotis macrocalyx
intro: Myosotis macrocalyx, commonly known as tall forget-me-not, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Myosotis macrocalyx include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Myosotis macrocalyx is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.

Myosotis macrocalyx is native to New Zealand, where it is considered a rare and endangered species. The classification of Myosotis macrocalyx includes the kingdom Plantae, and further broken down into the phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, and order Lamiales.
